reviews.review_description: AMAZING this was such a great experience something every single person that has a headset should experience! This is exactly what VR is supposed to be, and I pray to every single God out there that more games are made like this! I cannot wait to throw my money at the final game and I'm gonna tell every single person I know about how amazing this is because masterpieces like this  that the people follow your hands as you're doing things or that you can punch them in the face and they'll react and the fun little jokes and just everything about this is so amazing! to the people who worked on this and who are diligently working to get the full game out thank you so much for all of your hard work because people appreciate it and I love what you are working on I being one of them I can't wait to tell everybody about this! THANK YOUUU

For a game made by people who didnt have any money for the making of it I have to say this by far is my favorite vr game of all time. Ive been following this game’s progress for a little while and I loved playing the demo, one thing I do have to say though is the quality of the items in the bar are kinda low on the quest three unless you kinda get a little close or pick them up, same with the characters (a little)otherwise it Looked amazing! also idk if any of the creators of this game will read this. BUT PLEASE. DONT. ABONDON.THIS. MASTERPIECE. Seriously though this game in my opinion really has a chance at being one of the greats. Dont give up!

This was pretty good, especially for a small team with no budget initially. The only two things I hope get added are gender options (I'm a woman last time I checked) and voice acting (for immersion). But all in all a very good experience

It's so cool I can't imagine what the final product is going to be It's going to be so good I've played this game 5 time that's how good it is I love it I followed the creator I love the game good luck on the game do your best.

reviews.review_title: MAKE THE FULL GAME I LOVE THIS GAME

This game is a perfect example of VRs charm, something that could not be done in such an immersive way on any other platform. The world seems interesting, story has potential, and gameplay is super fun!! I’m pretty excited for the future of this project, keep up the good work guys!
Whens the full games release and will it be free like the demo this is a great amazing fun game and i would love to play the full game ty

Really polished gameplay, interesting characters, and pretty fun interactions, which is not what I expected after seeing this game getting clowned on for months on the subreddit because of the smoking mechanics. In the end, I did give the cat a smoke.

reviews.review_title: Great game just a few things
reviews.review_description: The game performed really well for a stand alone game. Just a few things I noticed was that one my second play through whenever I would serve a drink the glass would disappear I their hand. Also I noticed that you can’t do a thumbs up or a pointing motion with your fingers, I’m a person that likes to use hand gestures when playing vr so I was kinda disappointed when you couldn’t but other than that this demo is amazing.
Vibes, Story, Characters, Gameplay, 10/10, going straight to the kickstarter. Looking forward to the full game.
This game feels so good even on the quest 2 and is just a fresh take on thr cyberpunk theme. 10/10 stars if I could go that high. I will definitely be looking forward to the full release!!

Hopefully I can get the full game, but this game is amazing, although I might not be the best bartender, I think the game is spot on and could really be a good game for everyone to play, enjoy and clink your glasses.
Title's self explanatory. I had so much fun doing random stuff and getting repeatedly b*tch slapped by the boss for throwing soda cans at her. 10/10

reviews.review_title: Very good game but one thing
reviews.review_description: I need to see the full game but the full game COMES OUT IN 2027!!!!!!! Like thats crazy but i like the demo version but i dont know how much will the full game cost so guys see ya in 2027! Dj vr out!

I’ve been looking forward to this game for quite some time. But after this demo, my want has turned into a need. 

The potential for deep story line with comedic and romantic plots.
(Absolute cinema is all I can say.)
Please do not rush and keep doing what you can, I look forward to launch.
Ok

The object handling mechanics and  characters in this cyberpunk anime bartending game are excellent!

The core gameplay loop of mixing drinks and the attention to detail of the liquid system, coupled with the unfolding backstory in each character in the game leave me wanting more. If you’re a fan of visual novel anime games with a bit extra and love VR, this game is definitely for you.

Can’t wait to play the full release when it comes out!
if the demo is already so beautiful I can't imagine how it will be when it is released!
We ----> NEED <---- the full game amazing graphics and story and reality is amazing 5 🌟 🤩 ⭐️

reviews.review_title: Atmosphere so thick you can grab it!
reviews.review_description:
While normally it takes a while for me to be immersed in a movie/book/game, here in Project Mix that was instant. I think it does it thanks to setting the expectations properly, you are a bartender and this is a bartending game with extras [yes this is mainly a VN (Virtual Novel?) but the bar is its main hub]. The way how natural everything is when interacting impressed me and made me run to kickstarter to pledge. 

Even if there was no waifus, I would still insta buy and that is the best compliment an anime game can get :)
Needs better graphics for quest 3

After playing through the demo, I can say with certainty that I would've bought the full game on the spot if it was available. This bartending RPG experience is awesome. Every environment looks great. The art is fantastic. I can't wait to finish this story when the full game is available.

I cant wait to play the whole game. I'll even get my friends some copies when its out. 

I hope they have more physics implemented. When you move an item to the person in front of you,  they flinched but when you move your hand towards them it just goes through. The dialog is nice, wonder if its resizeable in the future. 

Really enjoyed the demo, i will replay it again multiple times. The moral choice you choose determines the response and outcome of the game so yeah, I'll be replaying it more.

Edit: after going on the credits, I went ahead to be  a backer at their kickstarter.
Can't wait for full game to come out

A really good base for an intriguing game, reminds me of the anime Black lagoon.

Well done for trying something new and I love the crappy apartment at the end where you can make noodles and smoke with your cat.

I hope you have many more plots in mind.

Edit; as others have said cigs go too fast

Let me start by saying that I've known about Project Mix for a while now. I followed the project for months leading up to the PC demo dropping after seeing it on Reels, and now, after playing the demo for the third time, this time on Quest, I can say that this game is groundbreaking. 

In my honest opinion, nothing in VR has reached this level of immersion. Starting with the Quest demo, it runs pretty great while still looking good. The limitations actually give it a charming, almost "cell-shaded" quality. As for the gameplay, bartending is so fun and satisfying. It's crazy to me how they have managed to get the feel of everything right. Pouring out drinks feels as if I were doing it in real life. The way that NPCs look at you while talking makes me feel as if there is a connection. And the rooftop and apartment scenes towards the end of the demo make me feel like I'm actually there. Watching a plane fly overhead, reading advertisements that could be for real products, it's insane world building. I want to LIVE in this city.  Plectrum Soft did a great job of  nailing that City Pop/1980s Asia vibe. 

I really can't wait for the full release, especially since Shoji Meguro, an artist known for his work on the music of the Persona series, will be doing the soundtrack. Plectrum Soft has lightning in a bottle, and if they can hold on to it, this game could be one of the best VR experiences out there.
The graphics and performance are very impressive. The haptic feedback from the controllers is implemented very well. Such a fun experience, so excited for the full game.

I love it so much, i can’t even begin to imagine what the full game will look like, till then, i’ll start brainstorming ideas for drinks. One thing i’d like to add though, it’d be cool if cigarettes burned a little slower, but that’s all i have to add, thank you!

I just got done playing the demo, and holy is amazing. I'm going to replay it like 20 times. I can't wait for the full game. I love the aesthetic and how calming it is. it is such an amazing idea. Even thinking about donating to the kickstart.
1000000000 that is the nuber i would rate it but it takes sooo much to dawnloww (sry e isnt my first launge)
I LOOOOVVVEEE BARTENDING!!!! even if this is just a demo, its still fun! cant wait for the full version.

Ngl, the storyline and characters really reel you in. The bartender job is hella fun to play. Instead of doing the only singular thing that most games do. This is a whole story mode game, go to work, build relationships and find hidden "secret" dialogue you can unlock depending what u make is awesome. The only thing that can propelled this even further, if they can. Add actual voice actors, add the option to actually talk with em, more interaction objects. Maybe even give the MC a cellphone for realism. This game has the potential to be a phenomenal game.
I was so excited I started throwing bottles at the customers... then I went home and played project mix 10/10

Maybe it’s the fact that I rarely play occupation simulator-like VR games, but for a short demo, this has been the most immersive and engaging vr game I’ve ever played. 

I’m someone who likes to try interacting with anything in the environment that I think I can interact with to see what are the random stuff I can do, and almost 100% of the time, I am able to interact with the things that caught my attention. IT IS SO SATISFYING AND IT LEVELS UP THE IMMERSION FOR ME. Even small interactions like opening the lighter with a physical flick, popping the bottle caps by thumping the bottle on the counter, to being able to pet the cat— all of them really sold the experience. I love the attention to detail.

The haptic feedback design is also very detailed. Animations look smooth and the drink making process is  really intuitive. Though when i pour drinks from the mixer, the liquid looks like it’s being held by an invisible lid on the mouth of the mixer.

Leaving the decision to make what drink fits best for a particular person’s needs gave me that sense of ownership and agency. I helped develop a small bartending mobile game project last year and this game reminded me of it so much and I think Project Mix is what the perfect VR version of it would’ve looked like😌

Can’t wait for the full version! Thank you!

I love this demo, the charaters and style. I haven't seen a bartender game on vr before and this concept is just great. I love how you can interact with alot of the  items like the fans in the apartment, fridges and even make yourself a cup of noodles. I also like how you can pet the cat, and even feed him some Ramen too!! I hope the devs get more money to make this game as in depth as they like, and for us players to enjoy the experience too. Id love it if they added drinking side affects too, along with maybe consequences if you slap or hit your customer. One thing id like to be a little easier is guessing drinks, maybe give a little more explanation as to what the customer would like (maybe im dumb and am just bad at guessing) but overall sooooo stoked to see the full release. Def following socials!!!

When I first saw this game on YouTube, I immediately felt it will be something special and will stood out from other games today (if you can even call them games anymore). It's also the first project I backed on Kickstarter, and I'm not disappointed.

The character models are superb, they look awesome and feel like pure art. It's great that each character expresses themselves differently. The "bartending mechanics" are clear and enjoyable. 

The entire environment feels authentic, and the interactivity around the player (regarding the current demo) is a lot of fun, which allowed me to get into the game very quickly. The music itself was pleasant to listen to, which also helps to immerse in the game's atmosphere. 

I feel like this is a project that will demonstrate all of us that you can still create amazing games with just passion and respect for the art, just like the whole game industry used to. (Long, long time ago sadly)

I can't wait for the game's official release.
Had a blast on this cant wait for it to be complete keep up the good work
I've only been into VR for a short time, and this is a game I really enjoyed after just 15 minutes of trying it. The only reason I stopped playing it was because the battery ran out... haha
Absolutely incredible demo, can't wait for the full game

I love the way it is story and gameplay togehter. I cant describe it but its soooo good
Amazing demo I can't wait for the full release im so glad this games getting backed up.

This game is addicting i love it, i hope the full version will be free

I try downloafing it on my vr but it dosent work if u know how to get the game help me pls
I really like the game you should make the full game pls I really fun
100\100 plz be more soon ill be looking forword to it 🔥🔥🔥

From the small bit this demo gives, I had a lot of fun. The gameplay is nice and satisfying, the character design is great, and the cyberpunk esthetic works well. It does what it sets out to do well, it’s a nice cozy game where you can make drinks.  
For criticism, the graphics could be better. I know the Quest 3 isn’t as powerful as your average VR headset (it’s basically the mobile version of VR) but I know the graphics could be better, I’ve seen better graphics with much more realistic art styles . It’s not a big issue, if the full game releases with the same graphics and textures I won’t be too upset. There were also a few sound effects I recognized and kinda broke my immersion. Again, it’s not a big deal and I’m sure they’re just placeholders.
Overall this game is great. Especially since according to the end this game was made with no budget, I’ll admit I was disappointed when the demo ended so soon, but that’s great on the developer’s part. I want more of this game, more of this world. Personally I cannot wait for the full game.

I recently tried out Project MIX, and it completely blew me away. It’s a VR bartending experience where you can mix all kinds of cocktails, and the level of immersion is incredible. From the way bottles clink to the sound of the shaker and the realistic liquid physics, everything feels surprisingly authentic.

What really stood out to me is the atmosphere — the bar has this cozy, stylish vibe that instantly pulls you in. The music, lighting, and small environmental details make it feel like a real place you’d love to hang out in.

The freedom to experiment with different ingredients is a lot of fun, and even simple actions like pouring or shaking feel satisfying. You can tell the developer put a lot of care into making the experience both relaxing and creative.

Even though it’s just a demo, Project MIX already feels polished and full of potential. If you enjoy VR experiences that focus on mood, creativity, and interaction, this one is a must-try. I can’t wait to see how it evolves!
The demo has so many mechanics and such an interesting concept! I think it could be the best anime VR game! It’s so fun to play on stream. I’m really looking forward to the full version >:3

This is by far the most anticipated VR game for me for a very long time if not ever! I'm a sucker for moody relaxed games, with great narrative and atmosphere. This has it all, jazzy music, great characters, great bartending mechanics. It reminds me of persona. I can't wait to see the full version and really hope this will be a 10-20+ hour game. Absolutely fantastic work so far. The only issue I have is the clipping of the glasses while the customers drink and the jaggies because of the lack of AA. I'm sure they will be able to iron that out. I'm heading to Kickstarter now and pledge as much as I can afford.

I have been excited for this demo for ages now. I was aware that the demo had been released on Steam and got very positive feedback, and I had watched a play through on YouTube (A Wolf in VR). It looked amazing then, and yesterday I was so excited to try it when I realised it had been released. 

I’m happy to say that it is really fantastic. Great atmosphere, great tactile gameplay with the bartending, and quirky/engaging characters. Also the best VR smoking I’ve experienced in a game (though I do agree with other reviews that cigarettes burn a bit too quickly).
